title: Investeraren om AI-rusningen: Inte oroad
summary_md:
# Investeraren om AI-rusningen: Inte oroad

**Feed**: dagen-med-di  
**Published**: 2026-06-18  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

The episode discusses the AI boom and whether it mirrors previous tech waves like the dotcom era; the guest (Robert) sees heavy FOMO and speculative capital but is not personally worried, though he wouldn't buy the richly-valued giants like OpenAI, which he says is bleeding cash and may run out around year-end. The conversation pivots to Swedish software/tech companies that sold off on fears of AI-generated code commoditising their products, arguing the sell-off was overdone given customer relationships, brands and know-how. The guest is constructively bullish on Swedish small/mid-cap tech and Sweden's entrepreneurial ecosystem.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 LIME — Lime | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 70%

Guest has bought Lime, calling it very good odds at ~P/E 20 (lowest since IPO) despite slower growth; strong margins, excellent cash flow, skilled chairman/CEO.

#### Reasoning

Sold off on overdone fears that AI-generated code commoditises software, but value lies in customer relationships, brands and know-how. Valuation at ~P/E 20 is the cheapest since listing. Growth has decelerated but margins and cash flow remain strong, with respected leadership (chairman Erik Syren). Guest sees an attractive risk/reward and has taken a position.

#### Sell Triggers

- if growth continues to deteriorate materially
- if margins or cash flow weaken
- if AI-generated software genuinely erodes its competitive moat

> *"Jag har köpt Lime. ... När du får Lime för typ P20, där den inte har varit alls sen de börsnoterades, så tycker jag att det är väldigt bra odds."*

---

### 🟠 VIT-B — Vitec | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 45%

Guest has looked a little at Vitec and finds it interesting after the sharp AI-driven sell-off in Swedish software names, but has not committed to a position.

#### Reasoning

Like Lime, Vitec fell sharply on the narrative that cheaper AI-generated code would eat software businesses. The guest views the 'software death' thesis as exaggerated and finds Vitec interesting, but has only looked at it lightly rather than buying, signalling a watch rather than a conviction position.

#### Sell Triggers

- if AI commoditisation thesis proves correct
- if valuation does not become attractive enough
- if fundamentals deteriorate

> *"Jag har tittat lite på Vitek. ... Larm i Vitek då, det är intressant."*

---
